{"key_event":"Jim Crow, labor conflict, and imperial expansion","reasoning":"Civil society remained strong through unions, Populists, immigrant associations, and party mobilization, but Black disfranchisement, Native suppression, strike defeats, and corporate consolidation reduced inclusive democratic constraint on power. State power rose as federal courts, injunctions, civil-service administration, coercive control in labor and Native conflicts, and the 1898 war/annexations expanded national capacity, despite still-limited economic regulation.","society_change":-0.3,"society_power":7.7,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":8.0}
